title: nephelium divaricatum
intro: Nephelium divaricatum, also known as the Ramaram or Ramaram fruit, is a tropical plant species native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its red or purple fruit and edible pulp. The medicinal properties of Nephelium divaricatum include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The health benefits of consuming the Ramaram fruit include reducing oxidative stress, improving immune function, and providing relief from respiratory problems. Nephelium divaricatum is typically prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or juice, and its pulp is also consumed fresh or dried as a dietary supplement.
